parent_request: Upgrade the local solar-system browser app into a fully 3D spacecraft experience.
goal: Deliver a local no-remote 3D solar system simulator that renders the Sun, major planets, and named spacecraft/stations with a spacecraft-focused interaction model.
context: Task 3 of the brain-hands solar 3D workflow plan requires issue seeding only, with no source changes to the app yet. The current app is a 2D canvas visual and needs a full spacecraft-oriented 3D upgrade plan.
implementation_steps: Replace the current visual pipeline with a full-bleed Three.js scene that includes a textured Sun, planets, orbital paths, and a depth-aware starfield backdrop.
Model all major planets plus spacecraft and stations: ISS, Tiangong, Hubble, Voyager 1, Voyager 2, New Horizons, Parker Solar Probe, Juno, Cassini, Rosetta, and Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter.
Create persistent scene controls for focus target selection, spacecraft visibility toggles, orbit labels, trails, time speed, and camera reset behavior.
Add interactive HUD for spacecraft and controls that remains legible at desktop and mobile sizes without overlap.
Implement focus-on-body and orbit-follow behaviors for both planets and spacecraft targets, with clear active target labeling.
Maintain reduced-motion support by disabling continuous animation while preserving a static nonblank scene, camera state, and target highlights.
Update the verify script to assert browser evidence is produced, and add required DOM hooks and status text expected by the browser checks.
Capture required screenshot artifacts for desktop and mobile, and document remaining caveats in the run report.
acceptance_criteria: App renders as a nonblank, full-bleed 3D scene with Sun, planets, orbital paths, and starfield in the browser.
All named spacecraft and stations are represented and focusable from the control surface.
Orbit lines, body labels, trails, and scene controls all work across desktop and mobile breakpoints.
Users can set camera target to spacecraft and planets and return to a stable default camera state.
No remote runtime assets are loaded; all models/data used for the scene come from in-repo assets or generated math/data definitions.
Reduced-motion mode renders a stable static 3D scene with no required animation dependence.
Verification captures at least desktop and mobile screenshots, and confirms key selectors are present.

manual_checks: Run local server command from browser checks at 127.0.0.1:5177 and open desktop and mobile viewports.
Confirm all spacecraft names appear and can be selected/focused.
Confirm no blocked mixed-content or remote network calls are required for rendering.
Verify visible labels, trails, and focus controls remain readable at narrow layouts.
Verify reduced-motion mode pauses animation while keeping the scene nonblank.

review_checklist: Do all spacecraft and stations exist in scene data with stable IDs and focus hooks.
Is the scene full-bleed and not contained by decorative cards.
Do all required controls mutate camera, simulation speed, and visibility states deterministically.
Is reduced-motion supported without relying on animation frames.
Do browser checks require only local server assets and expected selectors.
Are artifacts and browser evidence paths deterministic and committed.
risk_register: Three.js dependency install can fail on constrained environments; fallback to direct canvas/webgl paths may be required.
Mobile viewport constraints may cause control overlap without explicit density-aware layout work.
Large orbit scales can produce clipping if camera near/far planes are not tuned.
Spacecraft labels can become unreadable during close passes unless occlusion and depth handling are tuned.
handoff_prompt: Implement the 3D spacecraft upgrade with local static assets only. Keep controls responsive, add focus controls for all requested bodies, preserve reduced-motion behavior, and make verification deterministic with browser screenshot checks that record required selectors, console, and network expectations.
